Right now begin() refuses to nest (it raises 'transaction already open'). Make transactions nestable instead: begin() should push a new layer onto _layers even when one is already open, commit() should fold the innermost layer into its PARENT layer (or into _base when it was the outermost), and rollback() should discard only the innermost layer. Keep the existing _TOMBSTONE sentinel and the layer-stack design described in the module docstring. The seed tests in tests/test_seed.py must stay green.

Deletes inside nested transactions are still surfacing weirdly. Make delete() and tombstones fully nesting-aware: deleting a key inside a nested transaction must hide it from get() at that level and below; rolling back that nested transaction must make the key visible again with whatever value the parent/base had; and committing a nested transaction must propagate its tombstones up so the parent (or _base, if outermost) also forgets the key. A tombstone must NEVER leak into a sibling transaction that was opened after it was rolled back.

Add two read methods that respect the full transaction view: keys() returns the sorted list of currently-visible keys (honoring tombstones across every open layer), and items() returns the list of (key, value) pairs sorted by key for exactly those visible keys. Both must work whether or not a transaction is open, and must not include keys that are tombstoned at any visible layer.

Add an atomic counter op numincr(key, by=1): it reads the current visible integer value of key (treating a missing key as 0), adds `by`, and writes the result at the CURRENT transaction level (the innermost open layer, or _base if none). It must raise TypeError if the existing visible value is not an int. numincr must participate in transactions exactly like set(): a numincr done inside a nested tx that is later rolled back leaves the key at its pre-tx value.

Regression: the tombstone handling we added in step 2 corrupts state when a NESTED transaction aborts. Concretely: open tx A, delete an existing key inside A, then open a nested tx B, set that same key to a new value inside B, then rollback B. After rollback B, the key is wrongly reappearing with the OLD base value instead of staying deleted-by-A. Worse, if instead we numincr the key inside B and then rollback B, A's tombstone is sometimes lost and the base value leaks through on commit of A. Fix rollback so that aborting the innermost layer NEVER alters any other layer's writes or tombstones, and the parent layer's deletes/values are exactly what they were before the inner begin(). Do not weaken the step-2 or step-4 behavior.

Add snapshot(): return a plain dict of only the COMMITTED top-level data (i.e. _base), ignoring every currently-open uncommitted transaction layer, with tombstones already resolved (no _TOMBSTONE values, no deleted keys present). snapshot() must be a copy — mutating the returned dict must not affect the store. Confirm it ignores open nested transactions: if there are open layers with writes/deletes, snapshot() still reflects only what has actually been committed to _base.
